Numerator (Gap Closes When): Colonoscopy in 10yr, FIT in 1yr, or Cologuard in 3yr
Clinical Notes: FIT/Cologuard are easier alternatives to colonoscopy. Many patients avoid due to prep burden.
Triple-weighted. Mobile mammogram vans increase access. Digital breast tomosynthesis (3D mammo) also counts.

Full Name: Eye Exam for Patients with Diabetes
Numerator (Gap Closes When): Retinal eye exam in measurement year
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Retinal imaging telehealth counts in some plans. Annual requirement.

Numerator (Gap Closes When): Tdap or Td vaccine documented per ACIP schedule
Clinical Notes: Process measure, weight 1. Tdap booster every 10 years. Many adults unaware they need it.

Numerator (Gap Closes When): PCV20 or PCV15+PPSV23 per ACIP guidelines
Clinical Notes: New ACIP recommendation (2022): PCV20 alone is preferred. Simplifies from previous 2-dose regimen.
Requires 2 doses, 2-6 months apart. Retail price ~$200/dose but $0 under MA. Previous Zostavax does not count.

Numerator (Gap Closes When): Flu vaccine during current flu season (Aug 1 - Mar 31)
Clinical Notes: Annual measure. High-dose recommended for 65+. Walk-in pharmacies lowest barrier.
Triple-weighted. Home BP monitors with validated readings may count. Requires clinical documentation of BP value.

Eligible Population: Age 18-75 with diabetes AND hypertension, continuously enrolled
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Same BP target as CBP but only for patients with both conditions. Integrated care approach.

Key NDC/Drug Classes: Metformin, sulfonylureas, DPP-4i, GLP-1, SGLT2i, insulin
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. CMS uses < 8.0 (not < 7.0) to avoid hypoglycemia risk in elderly. Quarterly monitoring.

Numerator (Gap Closes When): Both eGFR AND uACR tests in measurement year
Clinical Notes: Process measure. Requires BOTH tests in same year. Home test kits for uACR available. Early CKD detection slows progression.

Eligible Population: Age 21+ with cardiovascular disease + statin Rx, continuously enrolled
Key NDC/Drug Classes: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors (statins): atorvastatin, rosuvastatin, simvastatin, pravastatin, etc. GPI: 3940xx
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted (returning 2027). PDC calculated from Part D PDE claims. 90-day mail order helps adherence. Side effect counseling critical.

Eligible Population: Age 18+ with diabetes + hypertension + ACE/ARB Rx, continuously enrolled
Key NDC/Drug Classes: ACE inhibitors (lisinopril, enalapril, ramipril); ARBs (losartan, valsartan, irbesartan). GPI: 3610xx, 3615xx
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Common side effects: ACE cough (switch to ARB), ARB dizziness. Auto-refill enrollment helps.

Eligible Population: Age 40-75 with diabetes + statin Rx, continuously enrolled
Key NDC/Drug Classes: Same statin NDCs as MAC but denominator is diabetes patients
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Overlaps with MAC for patients with both diabetes and CVD. Statin recommended for all diabetics 40-75 per ADA guidelines.

Numerator (Gap Closes When): Positive depression screen (PHQ-9 >= 10) with follow-up plan within 30 days
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. 30-day window is tight. Follow-up = documented plan (therapy referral, medication start, or additional assessment). Telehealth counts.

Eligible Population: Age 18+ with new depression episode, continuously enrolled
Numerator (Gap Closes When): PHQ-9 score < 5 (remission) within 12 months of diagnosis
Key NDC/Drug Classes: SSRIs, SNRIs, tricyclics, MAOIs, atypicals. GPI: 5810xx-5818xx
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Hardest measure to close. Requires actual clinical improvement. Medication adherence + therapy = best outcomes. Cut points are low.

Eligible Population: Age 18+ with new antidepressant dispensing + depression Dx, continuously enrolled
Numerator (Gap Closes When): PDC >= 80% through acute phase (84 days) AND continuation phase (180 days)
Key NDC/Drug Classes: SSRIs, SNRIs, tricyclics, MAOIs, bupropion, mirtazapine. GPI: 5810xx-5818xx
Clinical Notes: Triple-weighted. Two phases: acute (84 days from first fill) and continuation (180 days). Early discontinuation common at 4-6 weeks when side effects peak.

Eligible Population: Adults discharged from inpatient within 30 days, continuously enrolled
Numerator (Gap Closes When): Medication reconciliation completed within 30 days of discharge
Clinical Notes: Process measure. Requires documented medication reconciliation by prescriber or pharmacist within 30 days of IP discharge. Time-sensitive.
